name: Alpha-attractors inflation
summary: A class of inflation models with spontaneously broken conformal symmetry on the inflaton's Kahler manifold. The single parameter alpha controls the curvature of the moduli space; small alpha (large negative curvature) produces an attractor regime where slow-roll predictions are insensitive to potential details. Predicts n_s = 1 - 2/N and r = 12 alpha / N^2 — for alpha ~ 1 and N ~ 60 e-folds this gives r ~ 0.003, the same region of (n_s, r) as Starobinsky inflation.

addresses_puzzles.note: Predicts n_s ~ 0.965 and r in a controllable range 10^-4 - 10^-2 depending on alpha; the attractor regime makes the predictions insensitive to potential details, providing genuine universality. Squarely in the sweet spot of Planck + BICEP/Keck data.

touches_benchmarks.note: r in [10^-4, 10^-2] depending on alpha; LiteBIRD / CMB-S4 sensitivity (r ~ 10^-3) probes a large fraction of the alpha range.
n_s = 1 - 2/N ~ 0.965 for N=55-60, in agreement with Planck.

new_fields: inflaton on a hyperbolic Kahler manifold
introduces.note: Single new parameter alpha (curvature of the Kahler manifold). Potential shape becomes irrelevant in the attractor regime.

history: Kallosh and Linde (2013), 'Universality class in conformal inflation', JCAP 07, 002, arXiv:1306.5220. Family of attractors developed in subsequent papers; supergravity embeddings (Kallosh-Linde 2013, arXiv:1311.0472) and multi-field generalisations. One of the leading inflationary frameworks pre-LiteBIRD.

references.citation: Kallosh and Linde, 'Universality class in conformal inflation', JCAP 07, 002 (2013)
